intent: To design and develop a mobile fitness application that enables users to track physical activities, establish personal health goals, and monitor wellness progress over time.

entities.name: Mobile Client Application
entities.description: Cross-platform or native iOS and Android application that provides user interfaces for logging workouts, tracking calories, setting health goals, and managing subscriptions.

entities.name: Cloud Backend API Gateway
entities.description: The entry point for mobile client requests, orchestrating services such as user authentication, profile synchronization, activity logging, and goal tracking.

entities.name: Wearable Integration Service
entities.description: A dedicated service that connects to external wearable platform APIs, including Apple HealthKit, Google Fit, and Fitbit, to seamlessly import activity and wellness metrics.

entities.name: Central Database System
entities.description: A secure, cloud-hosted database storing user profiles, historical health metrics, logged exercises, subscription status, and premium training plans.

entities.name: Payment & Subscription Gateway
entities.description: An integration with Apple In-App Purchases, Google Play Billing, or third-party gateways to process payments for premium training plans and track freemium tier access.

architecture.description: The Mobile Client Application interacts with the Cloud Backend API Gateway to synchronize user data and access fitness features. Users log physical activities directly in the app or sync them automatically via the Wearable Integration Service, which pulls metrics from wearable devices. All activity logs, user profiles, and goal metrics are persisted in the Central Database System. Access to premium training plans is gated by the Payment & Subscription Gateway, which validates subscription statuses and updates the database accordingly to unlock paid features on the Mobile Client.

endpoints.summary: Register a new user account with the central backend.
validation_rules: email must be a valid email format
email must be unique in the database
password must be at least 8 characters long

endpoints.summary: Authenticate a user and return a JWT access token.
validation_rules: email is required and must match a registered user
password is required

endpoints.summary: Retrieve historical logged workouts for the authenticated user.
Log a physical activity workout directly or sync from an external service.
validation_rules: activity_type is required
duration_minutes must be a positive number
calories_burned must be a positive number
logged_at must be a valid ISO 8601 date-time string
source is required (e.g. 'manual', 'Apple HealthKit', 'Google Fit')

endpoints.summary: Link an external wearable platform integration (Apple HealthKit, Google Fit, Fitbit).
validation_rules: platform must be one of: 'Apple HealthKit', 'Google Fit', 'Fitbit'
access_token and refresh_token are optional but must be valid strings if present

endpoints.summary: Trigger synchronization of fitness and health metrics from a linked wearable platform.
validation_rules: platform must be registered and configured for the authenticated user

endpoints.summary: Retrieve historical health metrics such as daily step count, heart rate, or body weight.
Log a single health metric entry.
validation_rules: metric_type is required (e.g. 'steps', 'heart_rate', 'weight')
value must be a numeric value
unit must be a recognized health unit (e.g. 'steps', 'bpm', 'kg')
recorded_at must be a valid ISO 8601 date-time string

endpoints.summary: Retrieve the user's list of current and historical health and activity goals.
Create a new personal health or fitness goal.
validation_rules: goal_type is required (e.g. 'weight_loss', 'workout_count', 'calories_burned')
target_value must be positive real number
start_date must be a valid ISO 8601 date string
status must be one of: 'in_progress', 'completed', 'abandoned'

endpoints.summary: Update progress or details of an existing goal.
validation_rules: id in path must exist and belong to the user
current_value must be a positive number
status must be one of: 'in_progress', 'completed', 'abandoned'

endpoints.summary: Fetch available premium training plans and subscriptions.
Verify payment transaction and set up or unlock user premium status.
validation_rules: plan_id must exist in premium_plans database, or be null if general freemium to premium tier transition
tier must be one of: 'free', 'premium'
payment_gateway must be one of: 'Apple In-App Purchases', 'Google Play Billing', 'Stripe'
transaction_id must be unique and is required to verify the payment receipt

components.content: Welcome to active life. Register a new account or sign in to synchronize your fitness journey.
Track your weekly goals, calories burned, and daily steps count from all connected sources.
Heart Rate and Weight Progress Over Time
Select Fitbit, Apple HealthKit, or Google Fit integrations to connect, manage authorizations, or trigger manual sync operations.
Unlock advanced insights, daily coaching plans, and unlimited tracking tools.

rules.logic: When a user requests a premium training plan, the Cloud Backend API Gateway checks the subscription status in the Central Database. If the status is not active, it queries the Payment & Subscription Gateway for a real-time status update. Access is granted only if an active subscription is confirmed. Otherwise, access is denied and a checkout state transition is initiated.
triggers: Subscription renewal event from Payment Gateway
API request to fetch training materials

rules.logic: To maintain data integrity, the Cloud Backend API Gateway prevents duplicate activity entries. When a new activity log is received via the Wearable Integration Service or manual mobile entry, the system checks for existing logs for the same user with overlapping timestamps (within a 15-minute window) and identical activity types. If a duplicate is detected, the wearable-sourced metrics are merged with manual notes, and a single consolidated record is updated in the Central Database.
triggers: Batch sync request initiated by Mobile Client

rules.logic: Validate all physical and health metrics before persisting to the Central Database. Any payload with negative values for calories burned, duration, or distance is rejected. Heart rate values must fall within a biological range of 30 to 220 BPM, and the workout timestamp must not be in the future. Rejected payloads trigger an error alert back to the Mobile Client and are logged for debugging.
triggers: Wearable Integration Service pushes raw health data
